name: Set Up Storybook for Component-Driven Development
description: Install and configure Storybook to develop, document, and visually test React components in isolation.

steps.title: Initialize Storybook
steps.description: Run the Storybook initializer which auto-detects your framework and sets everything up.
steps.tip: Storybook auto-detects React, Next.js, Vite, or Webpack and installs the correct addons.

steps.title: Start Storybook dev server
steps.description: Launch the Storybook UI in the browser to view and interact with components.

steps.title: Create a story for a component
steps.description: Write a .stories.tsx file next to your component with different variants.

steps.title: Build static Storybook for deployment
steps.description: Generate a static site that can be deployed to any hosting provider.
steps.tip: Deploy the storybook-static/ directory to Vercel, Netlify, or Chromatic for team review.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Storybook Setup for React
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Initializing Storybook ==="
npx storybook@latest init --skip-install
pnpm install

echo "=== Verifying Storybook configuration ==="
ls -la .storybook/

echo ""
echo "=== Storybook Commands ==="
echo "Dev:   pnpm storybook"
echo "Build: pnpm build-storybook"
echo "Test:  pnpm test-storybook"
echo ""
echo "Create stories next to components:"
echo "  src/components/Button.tsx"
echo "  src/components/Button.stories.tsx"

faq.question: Does Storybook work with Next.js App Router?
faq.answer: Yes. Storybook 8+ has first-class Next.js support including App Router, next/image, next/font, and server actions mocking via the @storybook/nextjs framework.

faq.question: How do I add visual regression testing?
faq.answer: Use Chromatic (by the Storybook team) for cloud-based visual diffs, or install @storybook/test-runner for local Playwright-based screenshot tests.
